issue.body:
Iterations A and B shipped (see `ROADMAP-shipped.md` and the `drop cap` / `drop cap unrecovered` tags). Iteration C remains open: when body-word inference is ambiguous (e.g. body word `BELIEF` is already a valid English word, so the cursive fallback can't uniquely resolve the cap to `A`), look at the chapter title above to disambiguate (`A BELIEF IN OMENS...` -> cap is `A`).

Today these cases land in the `drop cap unrecovered` failure path and the closest body Word is tagged for human review.

Fixture exercising this gap: `tests/fixtures/layout_regression/inputs/footnotes-stacked-with-anchor` (cap `A`, body `BELIEF`).

Source: docs/ROADMAP.md (Drop-cap glyph recognition - Iteration C).
Spec: docs/specs/05-glyph-annotations.md

## Acceptance
- [ ] Heading-OCR cross-check implemented in the drop-cap pipeline
- [ ] `footnotes-stacked-with-anchor` fixture now resolves cap `A` against body `BELIEF` (no longer `drop cap unrecovered`)
- [ ] No regressions in existing drop-cap fixtures
- [ ] Disambiguation falls back to `drop cap unrecovered` when the heading line itself is unrecoverable (no false positives)

nodes.body: Triage decision: approved + needs-spec. Spec child issue: #48. Run `/spec-from-issue 48` to produce the design spec.

issue.title: Drop-cap recognition Iteration C: heading-OCR cross-check disambiguation

issue.body:
Tracks: #5

Tracks: #5

## Context

Parent feature-request: #5 (https://github.com/ConcaveTrillion/pd-book-tools/issues/5)

This spec issue tracks the design + decomposition of the parent feature-request via:

1. `/spec-from-issue 277940NEW` — produces `docs/specs/NN-<slug>.md` (the design spec) + draft PR.
2. `/decompose-spec docs/specs/NN-<slug>.md` — creates per-spec milestone + child issues with `bot:ship-issue-ready` where appropriate.

## Triage decision

Approved as needing-spec during the 2026-05-11 backlog triage sweep — this feature requires design work before implementation; not a one-PR tracking-style chore.

Inherits effort/model labels from the parent feature-request.

Spec: (to be filled by /spec-from-issue)

issue.body:
Approach: Import the script via importlib (same as test_decompose_spec_apply.py). Provide a `FakeGh` class and a `make_workspace(tmp_path)` helper that creates the canonical directory structure. Test each of the five deterministic actions plus the judgment-queue collection separately.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-17-gh-workflow-plan-c-grooming.md#groom-auto-tests
Verification: `uv run pytest tests/scripts/test_groom_auto.py -v` — all tests collected but FAILING (red bar) before Task 2.
Tracks: #57

Acceptance:
- [ ] `FakeGh` class has: `issue_view(repo, number)`, `issue_edit(repo, number, body)`, `issue_close(repo, number)`, `label_remove(repo, number, label)`, `label_add(repo, number, label)` — all record calls
- [ ] `make_workspace(tmp_path)` creates: `docs/superpowers/plans/`, `docs/superpowers/plans/archived/`, `docs/superpowers/specs/`, `docs/superpowers/decisions/`, `docs/superpowers/research/`, `docs/superpowers/research/archived/`
- [ ] `test_auto_unblock_removes_blocked_adds_ready` — task issue has `Blocked by: #5, #6`; both issues are CLOSED in FakeGh; assert `label_remove("status:blocked")` and `label_add("status:ready")` called
- [ ] `test_auto_unblock_skips_if_any_blocker_open` — task has `Blocked by: #5, #6`; issue #5 open; assert no label changes
- [ ] `test_milestone_complete_marks_plan_archived` — plan frontmatter `status: active`, milestone 100% closed in FakeGh; assert plan file moved to `archived/` and frontmatter `status: complete`
- [ ] `test_milestone_incomplete_no_archive` — milestone has 1 open issue; plan stays in place
- [ ] `test_spec_close_when_all_children_closed` — spec issue body has `Tracks: #10\nTracks: #11`; both closed; assert `issue_close` called on spec issue
- [ ] `test_spec_not_closed_if_child_open` — one child still open; assert no close
- [ ] `test_decision_closed_when_doc_written` — decision issue body has `Decision: docs/superpowers/decisions/2026-05-17-foo.md`; file exists with `Decision:` line; assert `issue_close` called
- [ ] `test_decision_not_closed_if_doc_missing` — file does not exist on disk; assert no close
- [ ] `test_research_archived_when_referenced` — research file `docs/superpowers/research/foo.md` referenced in a spec file; assert file moved to `archived/`
- [ ] `test_research_not_archived_when_unreferenced` — no spec/decision references it; stays in place
- [ ] `test_judgment_queue_collects_stale_decision` — decision issue open >14 days, no doc written; appears in `judgment_queue` with type `stale_decision`
- [ ] `test_judgment_queue_collects_orphan_plan` — plan frontmatter has no `synced:` date; appears in `judgment_queue` with type `orphan_plan`
- [ ] `test_judgment_queue_collects_stalled_task` — task `status:in-progress`, updated >7 days ago, no recent PR; appears with type `stalled_task`

milestone.description: GH Workflow Plan C — Grooming System
milestone.title: spec: gh-workflow-plan-c-grooming (#57)

issue.title: Write tests for groom-auto.py (TDD first)

issue.body:
Filed from the pd-book-tools deep code/security review.

Report: `docs/research/2026-05-22-deep-code-security-review.md`
Finding: #5

Moved from mistakenly filed meta issue: ConcaveTrillion/ocr-container-meta#298

Evidence:

- `pd_book_tools/geometry/bounding_box.py:638`
- `pd_book_tools/image_processing/cv2_processing/crop.py:35`
- `pd_book_tools/image_processing/cupy_processing/crop.py:31`

`crop_image()` and both `crop_to_rectangle()` backends clamp `minX/minY` to `width - 1` / `height - 1` before testing final overlap. A box entirely beyond the right or bottom edge can become a 1-pixel strip at the image border.

Impact:

- OCR cleanup/layout code can silently process unrelated border pixels instead of reporting no overlap.

Remediation:

- Test overlap before clamping.
- Clamp to `[0, width]` / `[0, height]`.
- Return `None` or a clear invalid-crop result for zero-overlap crops.
- Add tests parallel to `test_clamp_to_image_returns_none_when_outside_image()`.

---
This issue was filed from a read-only review. Confirm behavior with a focused failing test before implementation where practical.
issue.title: pd-book-tools: fully out-of-bounds crops can return unrelated edge strips
